title: Bridge Essay: 'Liking' and 'wanting' in eating and food reward: Brain mechanisms and clinical

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Water/Pleasure** (Phase 5) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Water/Pleasure to mechanistic neuroscience.

**'Liking' and 'wanting' in eating and food reward: Brain mechanisms and clinical implications.** — Physiol Behav. PMID: 32846152. DOI: 10.1016/j.physbeh.2020.113152. It is becoming clearer how neurobiological mechanisms generate 'liking' and 'wanting' components of food reward. Mesocorticolimbic mechanisms that enhance 'liking' include brain hedonic hotspots, which are specialized subregions that are uniquely able to causally amplify the hedonic impact of palatable tastes. Hedonic hotspots are found in nucleus accumbens medial shell, ventral pallidum, orbitofrontal cortex, insula cortex, and brainstem. In turn, amuch larger mesocorticolimbic circuitry gener
